guest: Garry Tan, CEO of Y Combinator

business_ideas.idea: LLM-powered automation replacing office workers and call centers
Workflow and eval testing for AI agents and language models
Personal brand and audience building as business moat

predictions.text: Large language models will create thousands of 100M-1B companies in next 2 years
AI will enable 20-person companies to generate billions in revenue
Corporate incumbents unprepared for disruption from agile AI startups

dynamics: Garry discusses YC selection philosophy, AI implications, and founder characteristics. Sam and Shaan ask probing questions about prediction models, AI limits, and business building strategies.
frameworks: Learn to cook loser - high agency approach to learning and problem solving
Make something people want - core YC philosophy transcends all eras
Bias to action - testing with users beats theorizing
Capital as a service - potential future model where data drives automated lending
learnings: YC's simplicity (10-min interviews, 7% equity) outperforms complex processes
Most successful founders are highly technical with strong work ethic and earnestness
AI will commoditize knowledge work, shifting value to execution and distribution
Personal brand and audience built through authentic communication drives startup success
Regulatory compliance software and automation of knowledge work have huge TAM

banger_quotes.quote: Learn to cook, loser - high agency approach to living
Simple things work - process complexity doesn't predict better outcomes
We're creating a new species just like AI will be
The spoon-bending party: you have to bend it with your hands

numbers_dropped: YC prints $3 billion in market cap per batch on average
Airbnb IPO generated $2B from under $20K seed investment (100x+ return)
YC per-employee value creation: ~$20M/year in market cap, ~$100M in total value created
Top decile YC investors: 16x return over 2-year period
Median investor in YC companies: 5x return (vs 3x typical VC)
notable: Deep insights into YC's selection process and philosophy; emphasis on simplicity beating complexity; AI seen as enabling new wave of startups
